summary: Verified the two P2 changes: (1) super_admin global view on GET /api/courts, /api/customers, /api/pricing with optional ?tenant_id= filter, and (2) FastAPI lifespan migration. Added /app/backend/tests/test_super_admin_global_view.py with 18 tests — all passed. Frontend smoke: super admin logs in, dashboard + Courts tab load without errors.

issues: The Courts page gates rendering behind a 'Select a venue' dropdown, so super_admin does not see cross-tenant courts on this screen without first picking a venue. Backend API now correctly returns all courts across tenants; the UI doesn't yet surface a 'no venue filter' / 'all tenants' view. Low priority UX enhancement, not a blocking bug.

passed_tests: GET /api/courts as super_admin (no tenant_id) aggregates courts from >=2 tenants (Elite + newly seeded TEST tenant)
GET /api/courts?tenant_id=<elite> as super_admin returns only Elite courts
GET /api/customers as super_admin returns list (may be empty) without error, no _id leak
GET /api/pricing as super_admin returns list without error, no _id leak
GET /api/courts as tenant_admin (elite@test.com) returns only Elite courts
GET /api/customers as tenant_admin returns only Elite customers
tenant_admin cannot bypass scoping by passing ?tenant_id=<other_tenant> (scoping still enforced by user.tenant_id)
Lifespan: backend logs show 'Spancle backend started'
Lifespan: super_admin seeded, bcrypt hash starts with $2b$, login works
Regression: GET /api/tenants, /api/venues, /api/bookings all return 200 for super_admin
Frontend: super admin /login -> /dashboard redirect works, Courts nav clickable, no page errors
critical_code_review_comments: routes/customers.py POST /customers does not enforce tenant_id from user for non-super_admin — a tenant_admin could POST with an arbitrary tenant_id in the body (or omit it entirely) and the document would be stored with whatever client sent. Compare with courts.py/qr.py which correctly override tenant_id from user context. Recommend mirroring the pattern: `if user['role'] != 'super_admin': customer_doc['tenant_id'] = user['tenant_id']` and require tenant_id for super_admin. Not tested as an exploit in this iteration but is a clear data-integrity gap.

routes/courts.py GET /courts accepts ?tenant_id from tenant_admin but silently ignores it (still scoped to user.tenant_id). This is safe but could be surprising; consider returning 400 if tenant_admin explicitly passes a different tenant_id, or at least document the behavior.
server.py lifespan: `client.close()` is sync on AsyncIOMotorClient in recent motor versions (returns None). Safe but consider `await client.close()` for clarity / future compatibility.
qr.py uses `bson.ObjectId` on user-supplied venue_id without try/except — passing a malformed id to /qr-code/{venue_id} will raise InvalidId -> 500 instead of 400/404. Minor.
Frontend Courts Management tab requires venue selection before rendering courts; for super_admin it would be useful to add an 'All tenants / all venues' view (now that the backend supports it).
test_credentials: admin@spancle.com / admin123 (super_admin) and elite@test.com / test123 (tenant_admin) — both verified working
seed_data_creation: Created a second TEST tenant (subdomain testsuper<ts>) with a TEST_V_ venue and TEST_Court_ court to prove cross-tenant aggregation on /api/courts. Cleanup fixture deletes all business_name=/^TEST/ tenants + their venues/courts/users and email=/^TEST_/ users after the module runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New test file: /app/backend/tests/test_super_admin_global_view.py (18 tests, ~3s). Run with: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_super_admin_global_view.py -v. Open code-review item worth flagging to main agent: POST /api/customers does NOT override tenant_id from user context (unlike courts/pricing). Consider adding a test that a tenant_admin cannot POST /customers with a different tenant_id.
rca of the issue: Not a bug fix iteration — validating two enhancements. (1) Courts/customers/pricing GET handlers now branch on role: super_admin either returns all docs or filters by optional ?tenant_id query param; other roles stay hard-scoped to user.tenant_id. Confirmed by creating a 2nd tenant + court and observing both appear for super_admin but not for elite@test.com. (2) Lifespan: @app.on_event deprecation replaced by @asynccontextmanager lifespan in server.py. Indexes + admin seeding verified via direct Mongo inspection; backend log shows 'Spancle backend started'.
